# Lwt

> Lwt is a concurrent programming library for OCaml. It provides a single data type: the *promise*, which is a value that will become resolved in the future. Creating a promise spawns a computation. When that computation is I/O, Lwt runs it in parallel with your OCaml code.

## Manual
- [Lwt](lwt/index.md)
- [Lwt manual](lwt/manual.md)
- [OCaml Package Documentation](index.md)
- [lwt\_direct index](lwt_direct/index.md)
- [lwt\_ppx index](lwt_ppx/index.md)
- [lwt\_ppx\_\_ppx\_let\_tests index](lwt_ppx__ppx_let_tests/index.md)
- [lwt\_react index](lwt_react/index.md)
- [lwt\_retry index](lwt_retry/index.md)
- [lwt\_runtime\_events index](lwt_runtime_events/index.md)
